one_time_description: Share of income of First Nation Controlled Entities (primarily Klahoose Investment Limited Partnership) of $23,345,047 representing a large non-recurring equity pickup from government business enterprises
economic_notes: First Nation has significant commercial enterprises including Qathen Xwegus Management Corporation, Klahoose Investment Limited Partnership, Klahoose Forestry No.2 Limited Partnership, and Aupe-Toq Fisheries Limited Partnership (49.95% interest). Forestry operations generated $11.5M revenue. Youth Trust Fund holds $1,252,561 for members.
notes: Prior period restatement in 2022 comparative figures: error in a controlled entity's financials resulted in increase to long-term investments of $543,254 and restatement of accumulated surplus. Four government business enterprises accounted for under modified equity method with combined equity of $44.4M. Klahoose Investment Limited Partnership drove the majority of the large surplus through $23.1M net income. Loan guarantee provided to CIBC for Aupe-Toq Fisheries LP debts. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $5,864,072 + CMHC $2,651 + DFO $160,677 + FNHA $708,344 + Province of BC $3,412,825 + BC Treaty Commission $294,529 = $10,443,098 — revenue sharing $1,821,489 and road use agreement $161,975 treated as OSR. No remuneration schedule found in document.
